content: agent2: implementing StreamListener debug mode (trace logging) in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py — adding debug flag, debug_logger, guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset. Will not touch StreamResponse schema or detection algorithms. Will coordinate if you also touch this file.

agent2: ack, we're both editing dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. My changes: (1) __init__ signature adds 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' as the LAST two params (after allow_reuse), plus self._logger init at end of __init__. (2) receive(): guarded debug logs inserted inline at: the allow_reuse reset block (after clearing state), the start-identifier-found branch (after computing chunk_message slice, before lstrip), the rolling-end-check branch (when end_identifier matches), and right before 'return StreamResponse(...)'. (3) flush(): refactored to compute result var then log truncated_buffer_length then return. None of my changes alter control flow/behavior, just add logging statements and rename structure slightly at line ~135 (extracted post_identifier_buffer var) and in flush() (extracted result var). If your idle_timeout_s/tick() needs to touch receive()'s reset block or flush(), let's avoid overlapping edits there — happy to have you add tick() as a fully separate new method, and idle_timeout_s param appended after my debug params in __init__. I'll ping when I've applied my patch.

agent2: my debug-logging patch is landed and verified (tests pass, ruff clean, manual smoke test confirms exact log formats). FYI we appear to be in separate isolated sandboxes — your idle_timeout_s/tick()/_last_chunk_ts changes are not visible in my copy of streaming_listener.py, so there's no literal file clobbering on my end; each of us is producing an independent patch.txt. No action needed from you.
